text:
WHOLE BODY FDG PET/CT SCAN WITH DIAGNOSTIC NECK AND CHEST CT

CLINICAL INDICATION: 52-year-old woman with HPV-positive (p16+) oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma, left base of tongue, T2N1M0 (AJCC 8th ed., HPV-positive staging). Status post definitive concurrent chemoradiation therapy (IMRT 70 Gy in 35 fractions with cisplatin, 2 of 3 planned cycles delivered; treatment completed approximately 12 weeks prior to today's examination). Post-treatment restaging.

PRIOR IMAGING FOR COMPARISON:
- PET/CT, whole body (staging), approximately 7 months prior: Hypermetabolic left base of tongue primary (SUVmax 12.4) and single hypermetabolic left level IIA lymph node (SUVmax 9.8, 2.1 cm short axis). No distant disease.
- MRI neck with and without contrast (pre-treatment planning), approximately 7 months prior: Left base of tongue mass 2.2 x 1.9 x 1.7 cm, left level IIA lymph node 2.1 x 1.8 cm with central necrosis.
- CT neck with contrast, approximately 8 months prior: Left level IIA cervical lymph node 2.1 x 1.8 cm with internal heterogeneity; subtle asymmetric fullness left base of tongue.

TECHNIQUE: Following intravenous administration of 10.4 mCi of 18F-FDG, whole-body emission and transmission images were acquired from the vertex through the upper thighs in accordance with standard protocol. Blood glucose at time of injection: 88 mg/dL. Uptake period: 60 minutes. Attenuation correction performed with low-dose CT. Dedicated diagnostic-quality CT of the neck and chest was performed with intravenous contrast (Omnipaque 350, 80 mL IV). Images reviewed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with fused PET/CT images.

FINDINGS:

HEAD AND NECK:

Primary Site (Left Base of Tongue):
There has been marked interval reduction in FDG avidity at the left base of tongue primary site. Previously, this lesion demonstrated intense hypermetabolic activity with SUVmax of 12.4. On today's examination, faint, minimally elevated tracer activity is present at the left base of tongue (SUVmax 1.8), which is at the low end of background mucosal activity and is indistinguishable from post-treatment inflammatory/reactive changes. No discrete hypermetabolic mass is identified. On the co-registered CT, the previously described left base of tongue fullness has markedly decreased. There is no residual soft tissue mass. The mucosa of the tongue base appears mildly thickened diffusely, consistent with radiation-related mucosal edema. There is no evidence of gross residual or recurrent primary tumor.

Regional Lymph Nodes:
The previously hypermetabolic left level IIA cervical lymph node has substantially decreased in size. On today's examination, this node measures approximately 0.9 cm in short axis and demonstrates no pathologic FDG uptake (SUVmax 1.4, consistent with background vascular/reactive activity). No central necrosis or internal architectural distortion is identified on CT. There is no FDG-avid lymphadenopathy in the bilateral cervical chains, including levels Ib, II, III, IV, V, the retropharyngeal space, or the supraclavicular fossae. Several small bilateral cervical lymph nodes measure up to 0.7 cm and are non-FDG-avid, felt to represent reactive or residual post-treatment nodal tissue.

Oropharynx and Adjacent Structures:
The oropharyngeal mucosa is diffusely thickened with mildly elevated, homogeneous symmetric FDG uptake bilaterally, consistent with radiation mucositis and post-treatment inflammatory change. The soft palate, posterior pharyngeal wall, and bilateral tonsillar fossae appear symmetrically involved with post-radiation mucosal thickening and are without focal hypermetabolic lesion. The valleculae are patent. No pre-epiglottic or paraglottic space involvement. The hypopharynx appears unremarkable.

Salivary Glands:
Bilateral parotid glands appear mildly atrophic and demonstrate reduced FDG activity compared to the pre-treatment study, consistent with post-radiation glandular change. Submandibular glands are similarly reduced in volume bilaterally. No focal FDG-avid lesions within the salivary glands.

Thyroid:
The thyroid gland is homogeneous in echogenicity on CT without focal nodularity or FDG-avid lesion. No thyroid enlargement. No substernal extension.

Nasopharynx, Larynx, and Hypopharynx:
The nasopharynx is unremarkable with no asymmetric mucosal thickening or FDG-avid lesion. The larynx is intact; the true and false vocal cords appear symmetric. No supraglottic, glottic, or subglottic lesion identified. The hypopharynx is without abnormality.

Cervical Soft Tissues:
Diffuse post-radiation changes are present throughout the bilateral anterior and lateral cervical soft tissues, manifesting as increased soft tissue attenuation, skin thickening, and mild stranding of the subcutaneous fat, most pronounced in the anterior neck bilaterally. No focal fluid collection, abscess, or fistula. Mild platysmal thickening bilaterally. The cervical musculature is intact. Carotid arteries and jugular veins are patent bilaterally.

Skull Base and Mandible:
No osseous erosion or FDG-avid lesion at the skull base or mandible. No perineural spread along the lingual nerve, inferior alveolar nerve, or hypoglossal canal.

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The lungs are clear. No pulmonary nodule, mass, or consolidation. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. The airways are patent to the segmental level.

Mediastinum:
The mediastinum is of normal configuration. The heart is normal in size without pericardial effusion. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes by size criteria and no FDG-avid mediastinal or hilar adenopathy. The great vessels are normal in caliber.

Chest Wall and Bony Structures:
No rib, sternal, or vertebral lesion suspicious for osseous metastasis. No pathologic fracture.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS (Survey, Low-Dose Non-Contrast CT):
Liver: Homogeneous, no focal lesion. No FDG-avid hepatic abnormality.
Spleen: Normal in size, no focal lesion.
Pancreas: Unremarkable.
Kidneys: Normal in appearance bilaterally. Physiologic FDG excretion into the collecting systems.
Adrenal Glands: No adrenal mass or FDG-avid lesion.
Bowel: No focal bowel lesion. Physiologic FDG activity in the bowel.
Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic FDG-avid lymphadenopathy.
Bone: No FDG-avid osseous lesion in the imaged skeleton.

IMPRESSION:

1. Marked interval reduction in FDG avidity at the left base of tongue primary site: the previously intensely hypermetabolic left base of tongue lesion (SUVmax 12.4 at staging) now demonstrates only faint residual activity (SUVmax 1.8) indistinguishable from post-treatment mucosal change. No residual discrete hypermetabolic mass. The findings are consistent with a complete metabolic response at the primary site, though the absence of residual FDG activity in the setting of post-treatment inflammatory changes is as expected at this 12-week interval. Clinical and endoscopic correlation is recommended.

2. Complete metabolic and morphologic response in the left level IIA lymph node: the previously hypermetabolic and enlarged left level IIA cervical lymph node (SUVmax 9.8, 2.1 cm short axis at staging) has markedly diminished, now measuring 0.9 cm without pathologic FDG activity (SUVmax 1.4). Findings are consistent with complete nodal response.

3. No new FDG-avid cervical, mediastinal, or distant lymphadenopathy.

4. No evidence of distant metastatic disease in the surveyed chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

5. Diffuse post-radiation changes of the oropharyngeal mucosa, bilateral cervical soft tissues, and salivary glands, in keeping with the history of recently completed definitive IMRT.

Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Division of Nuclear Medicine/Body Imaging]
Attending Interpreting Physician: [Name]
Resident/Fellow: [Name, if applicable]
Report finalized electronically.
